reviews.review_title: Good game with room for improvement
reviews.review_description: I like the environment the game puts you in inside the sub and all the submarine sounds around you. It makes everything seem real like you are really there. The views through the periscope underwater and when you are looking out over the ocean attacking the ships at night are pretty cool.  It would have been nice to be able to grab the periscope, the steering wheel, and dive lever with your hands, if it was available on the Oculus Go. I agree with the other review that maybe more work could be done to add variety to the gameplay and flesh out the design and backstory of the game a little more. All in all, I think this is a pretty good game!

reviews.review_title: It's not what it looks like
reviews.review_description: It's not what it looks like. If you are thinking of a submarine game like famous PC games, do not... this one has nothing to do with it... it's just a succession of scenes that you have to go through until you shoot the torpedoes. If you sink enough ships, and avoid depth charges, (with a system in which you can't even fix the depth well, because either it goes up or down, but it doesn't stay fixed), you continue with another mission and the same succession of scenes until you fire the torpedoes. If you don't sink enough ships, there comes a scene where you're sunk. That's all. And you always have to go through the same things, the most ridiculous: detect (rather distinguish the sound of the machines) with a device the sound of enemy engines, among 6 sounds that are always the same, whales included. You don't run the submarine, you don't sail anywhere, you don't approach the ships... they're just scenes you have to go through until you drop the torpedoes. That's all.
